_comment: SCENARIO DESIGN — 1 PRB = 1.2 Mbps, total SLA = 60 Mbps
gNB 223 needs 25 PRBs  (ue3=15 + ue4=9 + ue5=6 Mbps)
Phase 1  (0–49)   : Both healthy ~35 PRB — warmup, no moves, cost-rebalance only
Phase 2  (50–99)  : gNB 21 tight ~22 PRB, 223 surplus ~40 — SWAP expected
Phase 3  (100–149): gNB 21 severe ~16 PRB, 223 abundant ~42 — DIRECT GHOST MOVE expected
Phase 4  (150–229): Both abundant ~45 PRB — SLA satisfied, COST REBALANCE dominant
Phase 5  (230–299): gNB 223 tight ~18 PRB, 21 abundant ~42 — SWAP or DIRECT MOVE back

phases.name: P1: warmup — both healthy 35 PRB, no deficit
phases._comment: Both gNBs at 35 PRB >> 25 PRBs needed. Broker warms up, cost-rebalance only. No UE moves expected.

phases.name: P2: gNB 21 tightens to 22 PRB — SWAP window
phases._comment: 21 drops to 22 PRB, can only serve ~26 Mbps. Needs 30. Deficit ~4 Mbps. 223 stays at 40 PRB (headroom 15 Mbps). Broker should SWAP: reduce ue2 rate on 21, increase ue4/ue3 on 223.

phases.name: P3: gNB 21 severe 15-16 PRB — DIRECT GHOST MOVE window
phases._comment: 21 drops to 16 PRB, can only serve ~19 Mbps. Deficit ~11 Mbps. Too large for swap alone. 223 at 42 PRB (headroom 20 Mbps, can absorb a full UE). Broker should trigger ghost move of ue2 (20 Mbps) from 21 to 223.

phases.name: P4: both abundant 45+ PRB — COST REBALANCE dominant
phases._comment: Both gNBs well above 25 PRBs needed. No SLA deficit. Broker sees gNB 21 is expensive (cap_base=25, nu=1.3) vs 223 (cap_base=25, nu=1.0). Should shift load toward cheaper gNB 223.

phases.name: P5: gNB 223 tightens to 18 PRB — SWAP or DIRECT MOVE in reverse
phases._comment: Now 223 drops to 18 PRB, can only serve ~22 Mbps. Needs 30. Deficit ~8 Mbps. gNB 21 at 42 PRB has headroom. Broker should move load back to 21 via swap or ghost move.

_comment: gNB 21: expensive — high nu=1.3, high pi_min. Scarcity hits hard when cap is low.
gNB 223: cheaper — lower nu=1.0, lower pi_min. Broker prefers 223 when both healthy.
This asymmetry makes cost-rebalancing visible in Phase 4:
even when both gNBs have surplus, broker shifts load from 21 to 223 to reduce cost.
